description: Phase 4: Developer-facing map editor (gated to NODE_ENV=development, tree-shaken from production), 2D map overlay, balance tuning passes, edge case hardening, visual polish, and final quality gate (pnpm lint/typecheck/build). Depends on PRD-01, PRD-02, PRD-03.

userStories.title: Map editor mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to edit maps visually so I can design levels without writing JSON by hand.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+E toggles map editor mode only when process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'.
Entering editor mode: game pauses, camera switches to free mode (WASD pans, mouse rotates, scroll zooms).
Exiting editor mode: game resumes, camera returns to follow mode.
Editor active indicator: prominent 'EDITOR MODE' banner visible at all times while editor is active (prevents confusion with gameplay keybindings — M, D, 1-9 are remapped in editor context).
Editor does NOT destroy existing game state — entities persist while editing.
src/game/maps/MapEditor.ts exposes enable(), disable(), isActive(): boolean, getMapData(): MapData, loadMapData(data: MapData).
Editor code is gated at construction so it tree-shakes from production bundles (same pattern as the debug menu in PRD-03).
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Map editor tools — terrain
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to place and modify terrain blocks so I can shape the map geometry.
acceptanceCriteria: Tool palette in side panel: Grass block, Dirt block, Stone block, Water zone.
Select tool -> click on map -> block placed at grid-snapped position (1u grid).
Terrain blocks have adjustable height (side panel input: 0.5u to 5u in 0.5u increments).
Click existing block -> select it -> side panel shows editable properties (type, height, position).
Delete key removes selected block.
Water zone: click-drag to define rectangular water area, depth input in side panel.
Visual feedback: ghost preview of block before placement, highlighted border on selected block.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Map editor tools — entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to place spawn points, resource nodes, and special zones on the map.
acceptanceCriteria: Entity tools in palette: Player Spawn, Cat Spawn, Resource Node (Grass/Sticks/Water), Hidden Terrain Zone, Yarn Pickup.
Player Spawn: only one allowed — placing a new one moves the existing one.
Cat Spawn: place multiple, each shows as a cat icon marker.
Resource Node: select type from dropdown, place on map, configure respawn time in side panel.
Hidden Terrain Zone: click-drag to define area, set height in side panel.
Yarn Pickup: place collectible, set yarn amount in side panel (default 3).
All placed entities show as distinct colored markers in editor view.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Map editor — move & delete
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to move and delete placed objects so I can iterate on map layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Move tool: click entity -> drag to new position -> release to place (grid-snapped).
Delete tool: click entity -> entity removed from map.
Keyboard shortcuts: M for move, D for delete, 1-9 for tool palette selection.
Undo: NOT required for MVP (explicitly out of scope).
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Map editor — save & load
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to save map layouts as JSON files and load them so maps are portable and versioned.
acceptanceCriteria: Save button: exports current map state as MapData JSON (browser download dialog).
Filename auto-generated: map-[name]-[timestamp].json.
Load button: file picker -> select JSON -> MapEditor.loadMapData(parsed) -> map rebuilds.
Loaded map replaces current map entirely (all entities destroyed and recreated).
'Play' button in editor: calls MapManager.loadMap(editorMapData) -> exits editor -> game resumes on the edited map.
Invalid JSON shows error message (does not crash). Validate via Zod schema mirroring MapData.
No server upload in MVP. Maps live as files on the developer's machine and are committed alongside code (e.g., src/game/maps/TestMap.ts regenerated by hand). Server-side map storage is deferred.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: 2D map overlay
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see an overhead 2D map so I can orient myself in the world.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/ui/NavigationOverlay.ts renders when M key pressed (gameplay mode only — M is remapped to Move tool when Map Editor is active per US-304).
2D top-down view rendered as colored regions: Grass green, Dirt brown, Stone gray, Water blue. Hidden terrain not shown unless currently revealed by Curiosity Cat.
Player icon: colored dot matching character color, with arrow showing facing direction.
Active cats: small cat-shaped icons.
Resource nodes: small icons distinguishable by color per type.
Map scales to fit overlay panel (fixed size, e.g., 400x400px centered on screen).
Pressing M again or Escape closes the overlay.
Game continues running behind the overlay (NOT paused) — input is consumed by the overlay while open.
Overlay rendered on a separate HTML5 <canvas> over the game canvas (no Three.js involvement).
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Movement balance pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify and tune all movement values so traversal feels satisfying.
acceptanceCriteria: Walk speed (4.5 u/s) tested: traversal feels purposeful, not sluggish.
Acceleration (0.3s) tested: start feels responsive but weighted.
Deceleration (0.2s) tested: stopping feels controlled, not slippery.
Jump impulse (3.5u) tested: reaches intended platforms, feels powerful.
Coyote time (5 frames) tested: jumping after walking off edges works reliably.
Jump buffer (5 frames) tested: rapid repeated jumps feel responsive.
Air control (70%) tested: can steer mid-air without ice-physics feel.
Swimming speeds tested: surface (3.2 u/s), dive (2.0 u/s), ascend (2.5 u/s).
Any values that feel wrong are adjusted in src/game/config.ts with a comment explaining the change.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Cat balance pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify and tune cat costs and effects so each cat feels worth its yarn cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Loaf (1 yarn): height appropriate for stepping; test stacking 2-3 to reach platforms.
Zoomies (2 yarn): trail length and 2x multiplier feel impactful but not game-breaking.
Curiosity Cat (2 yarn): 5u reveal radius useful, 20s duration sufficient.
Pounce (3 yarn): 3.5u launch from cat's resting height — total reach = cat height + impulse apex. Reliably reaches High tier (~3.5u). 3-yarn cost feels justified vs. free 1.2u base jump.
Starting yarn (10) tested: enough to experiment with all cats in a single session.
Cat limit (3 for MVP) tested: creates meaningful placement decisions. Note: Map & Movement System spec allows 3-5 scaling with progression — keep runtimeConfig tunable for post-MVP.
Any values that feel wrong are adjusted in cat definition files with comments.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Resource balance pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want resource gathering to hit the GDD feel targets so the core loop is satisfying.
acceptanceCriteria: Grass gather time (1.5s) tested: feels 'snappy — instant gratification' per GDD.
Sticks gather time (1.5s) tested: comparable feel to grass.
Water gather time (2.0s) tested: slightly slower, feels like a higher-value resource.
Respawn timers tested: Grass (30s), Sticks (45s), Water (60s).
Inventory cap (10) tested: tension without frustration. If >20% of session is inventory management, increase to 15.
Resource distribution: player can gather 5-10 Grass, 2-3 Sticks, 1 Water per 10-minute session (per Balance Spreadsheet target).
'+1 Resource' floating text feedback feels rewarding.
Any values adjusted are documented in comments.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Edge case fixes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all known edge cases handled gracefully so the game never enters an unrecoverable state.
acceptanceCriteria: Player cannot get stuck in terrain (push-out resolution if clipping occurs).
Player cannot walk out of map bounds (collision walls at edges).
Cat cannot be summoned inside collision geometry (auto-raise per spec: 3 attempts, then fail with feedback).
Cat dismissed while player is standing on it: player falls safely (no void/clip).
Oxygen draining to 0 -> health draining to 0: player respawns at map spawn point with full health (5 hearts) and full oxygen — no death screen, no health penalty (Zen Engagement pillar).
Inventory full -> gathering attempt: clear feedback message, gathering does not start.
Saving while swimming: on load, if player position is in water, SwimmingState is correctly applied.
Tab unfocus: game pauses (requestAnimationFrame stops naturally), auto-save still fires via the beforeunload beacon (PRD-03 US-203).
Rapid input: holding multiple keys, mashing jump, spam-clicking cat summon — no crashes or inconsistent state.
HMR / route change: navigating from /play to / and back leaks no WebGL contexts (verify via chrome://gpu). Game.destroy() from PRD-01 handles this.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Visual polish
userStories.description: As a developer, I want visual refinements that make the game feel more alive without adding art assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Ground plane shadows: player and cat entities cast elliptical shadows onto ground.
Terrain grid lines: subtle grid overlay for spatial reference.
Terrain color variation: slight random tint within terrain types (not all grass identical green).
Water zone: semi-transparent blue with subtle animation (opacity pulse or UV shift).
Cat summon effect: brief scale-up animation (0 -> 1 over 0.2s).
Cat dismiss effect: brief scale-down animation (1 -> 0 over 0.2s).
Gather progress: smooth progress bar (no jerky step increments).
Hidden terrain reveal: shimmer/fade-in transition.
pnpm typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Final quality gate
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all quality checks passing so the MVP is shippable.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm lint passes with zero warnings.
pnpm typecheck passes with zero errors.
pnpm build succeeds and produces an optimized bundle.
No console errors on clean startup of /, /play, or the auth callback.
No console warnings related to game logic (Three.js deprecation warnings acceptable).
Game maintains 60 FPS with test map fully populated (all resource nodes, 3 active cats, swimming active).
Auth flow completes successfully (sign in -> /play, sign out -> /, refresh -> session persists via WorkOS cookies).
Save/load round trip verified (play -> close tab -> reopen -> state matches).
All 4 cat types functional (verified per PRD-02).
Swimming + oxygen verified (verified per PRD-02).
Debug menu functional (verified per PRD-03) and stripped from production bundle (grep production JS for 'DebugMenu' — should not appear).
Map editor produces valid MapData (save -> load -> identical map) and is similarly stripped from production.
